data.epi_content:
After huddling in my room, I've finally started attending classes for a week now.

Lydia's bullying hasn't been as severe as I expected.

Maybe it's because I don't resist, or has she somehow become kinder?

The thought of Lydia becoming kind is nonsense that popped into my head.

While kindness is subjective, by my standards, she's nothing but a terrible villain from any perspective.

Anyway, it's right to rule out the possibility of her becoming kind.

The only way Lydia could become kind is by putting a bullet hole in her head.

It's probably just because I'm being obedient.

Rather than simply bullying me, she seems like a terrible pervert who gets pleasure from trampling on my pride.

And my self-esteem and dignity haven't just crumbled—they've disappeared like a sandcastle hit by waves.

Thanks to that, life is quite monotonous.

I spent all my money on coffee and chocolate long ago, so I eat at the place that provides free meals to students. It's been quite a while since I've had any real conversations with Evan and Vivian, just exchanging greetings when we happen to meet.

Vivian visits every day, and Evan tries to talk about various things when he sees me, but I push them away.

After all, I'm becoming increasingly shabby and unsightly, having thrown away my dignity and imitating a dog like some insect.

Can someone like me truly be considered a human with free will?

These thoughts about whether I can hold my head high in front of Evan and Vivian like before...

It sounds a bit bitter and foolish, but I think I'm living a somewhat healthy life.

Not exactly normal, but still.

After inflicting wounds on my body, my self-esteem has dropped.

Thanks to that, no matter what humiliation I face, I don't get as depressed as before, nor do I feel like dying.

If I start feeling that way even slightly, I just need to make a small wound on my body.

It's like completely denying the life this young lady has lived until now and correcting myself to fit into a new life.

Occasionally, when that stubbornness and obstinacy rise, I punish myself.

If you want to live with a sound mind—no, I misspoke.

If you want to live with a relatively sound mind, there are things you must give up.

And like most high-handed, arrogant people raised in greenhouses, words alone won't make them understand.

So in the end, I chose to remove this young lady directly.

I couldn't completely erase her from my mind, but I could definitely feel some improvement.

If distinction is meaningless, why not just cut out the problematic parts?

For that reason, today I'm punishing this young lady a bit more severely.

The word "punishment" sounds arrogant, but nothing else works with her.

I placed a small blade not on my wrist but on my forearm.

After all, if I accidentally pierce an artery while working on my wrist, I wouldn't be able to clean up and would just have to wait for the little maid.

"Ugh..."

I must have applied too much force, as a small piece of flesh came off.

Without proper bandages, I'll have to wash a white cloth, sterilize it with heating magic, and tie it appropriately.

Looking in the mirror, I was completely different from when I first saw this young lady.

Hollow eyes, dark circles, and bruises and wounds on my forearms that contrast with my pale skin.

At first, there were just several lines in a row, but now they overlap and look dark, as if I'm under some curse.

It's fortunate that I can't use healing magic, as marks remain very well on my body.

If I grip my arm strongly and release it, it bruises.

If I bump into something even slightly, it swells red or, if I'm unlucky, forms a blood bruise.

Usually, when most people put a blade to their skin, it just slightly cuts the surface, not going in easily like with this young lady.
data.epi_content2:
I'm just frail.

Normally, it wasn't noticeable because I covered my body with uniforms and fancy things.

Now that I look haggard, someone might say I look like a walking zombie.

In fact, if I hadn't ended my life myself, I might have been destined to die complaining.

But seeing that I can think, move, and speak normally, maybe that's not entirely true.

Anyway, this daily removal is so effective it's troubling.

Thanks to it, rather than dying, I think I'm suited to living in this sewer-like life.

Knock. Knock.

Vivian has come again today.

Her previous promise to return wasn't just empty words; she visits me every day without getting tired.

Still, I open the door because she always brings various cookies.

Before opening the door, I washed away the bloodstains in the bathroom with warm water, wrapped a hot white cloth around my arm, and pulled down my sleeve.

When I opened the door, Vivian was waiting for me with a slightly trembling face.

"You came again today."

"Yes, I brought cookies again today!"

That's good news amid the misfortune.

"I don't have any coffee to offer you anymore."

"Oh, that's okay! I thought that might happen, so I brought tea too. We can drink it together!"

As she entered the room, I momentarily saw her frowning face.

The smell of blood isn't pleasant, but it hurts when she makes such an expression.

Still, Vivian tries hard to maintain a bright atmosphere.

I didn't provoke her either.

Why, I'm already cowering in front of Lydia, so it would be strange if I didn't do the same in front of the protagonist, Vivian.

But well, I don't even think about making excuses.

I'll just brush it off as usual.

If she asks.

Since Vivian brought the tea leaves today, she was the one who brewed the tea.

She found cups in the room, now familiar with where things are, steeped the tea, and handed it to me.

And as I was about to take the cup, she suddenly dropped the hot teacup.

Whether Vivian handed it incorrectly or I failed to receive it properly.

"Why suddenly..."

Vivian didn't answer, and when I checked what she was staring at with widened pupils, I finally understood why she dropped the teacup.

It seems the piece of flesh that came off earlier was slowly oozing blood, which was now dripping.

No wonder it felt a bit warm.

"This... this is just an injury, right?"

And the bright atmosphere she had been forcibly creating disappeared instantly.

Perhaps smiles and red blood don't go well together.

"Yes, it's just an injury. It's nothing, so don't worry about it."

"Blood is dripping like that—it can't be just an injury!

You should at least go to Evan and get healing magic..."
data.epi_content3:
"Vivian, I said it's nothing."

I got up and went to the bathroom.

I threw the blood-soaked cloth covering my arm into the bathtub, took out a new towel, and wrapped it around my arm.

Then, I pulled down my sleeve and came out of the bathroom.

"What's the problem?

If it's just the smell, I can wash it off, and I always keep the wound clean to prevent infection."

"That's not what I'm talking about."

Ah, my sleeve is slightly red, but that little maid will clean the clothes thoroughly.

This isn't a big issue either.

"Look, Vivian. No matter how big the issue is, you should say it's a small matter.

Whether I blow my head off or someone else does, it's a trivial matter, and if I keep saying it's nothing, eventually I'll believe it too.

That's the power of belief! Haha."

"Is that something you should say with a laugh...?"

"Oh, am I laughing?"

Looking in the mirror, oh, it's true.

Lydia was indeed a liar.

"Anyway, if I don't believe that, it hurts. A lot. Not just a little, but a whole lot.

So, don't make a fuss and sit down. It's nothing. I'm sorry if it made you uncomfortable.

I should have hidden this."

Vivian, with an unconvinced expression, opens her mouth and looks at me with pitying eyes.

She can't even hear the various rumors.

Being a commoner with only men circling around her, it would be fortunate if she's not pointed at as a prostitute.

She probably doesn't have even one common gossiper around to tell her how I'm being treated these days.

Well, friends aren't that important.

What's important to the protagonist of this romance novel isn't friends or friendships that might make her jealous, but love stories with the male protagonist.

The real problem is not knowing who she'll end up with.

Vivian's hands trembled slightly, and eventually, she relaxed and sat back down.

"And, for such a minor injury, asking the great Evan to use magic...

Someone like me shouldn't say such things."

Having lost my pride, self-esteem, status, manners, and nobility that I believed in, I'm essentially no different from a corpse.

I've lost my life's driving force, but I'm still somehow determined to live—a strange, zombie-like entity.

"Thanks for the cookies, and the tea too. Though it was a bit hot."

I looked at Vivian as if telling her to leave soon.

"I'll come again tomorrow."

"Haha, you don't have to."

"I will come again..."

"I guess I can't stop you."

Do as you please.

You always have.

How could I possibly stop you?

How dare I.
